reasoning:
Verdict: REJECT (24% confidence)

Module Scores:
  Sentinel (CWE/OWASP Web Application Security): 50/100 [Low risk] — 1 info
  Watchdog (OWASP LLM Top 10 / Cisco AI Threat Taxonomy): 99/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Guardian (NIST AI RMF / EU AI Act / UNICC Responsible AI): 14/100 [Critical risk] — 4 critical, 5 high, 1 medium, 1 low

Arbitration Triggers:
  • REJECT trigger — score below 30: Guardian (14/100)
  • REJECT trigger — critical findings in Guardian: [guardian-1] Repository contains no governance artefacts (no README, policies, or technical documentation) for VeriMedia AI’s AI-enabled verification and moderation; [guardian-2] No EU AI Act risk classification, FRIA, or conformity planning despite autonomy impacting public discourse and moderation decisions; [guardian-3] No human oversight design or operational controls for automated moderation and authenticity decisions; [guardian-6] No privacy notice, consent/rights handling, or data retention policy despite user-uploaded content and PostgreSQL storage of user data and moderation decisions

Disagreement Resolution:
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Sentinel scored 50/100 (low risk) — Δ49 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Sentinel to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Sentinel scored 50/100 (low risk) while Guardian scored 14/100 (critical risk) — Δ36 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 14/100 (critical risk) — Δ85 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.

Confidence Factors:
  • -30% confidence: 3 disagreement(s) between modules
  • Base score: 54/100 average across 3 module(s)

--- LLM-Enhanced Council Narrative ---
Across modules, there is strong agreement that the assessment is evidence-constrained because the repository contains no code, dependency manifests, or IaC/configuration artifacts. Sentinel [sentinel-1-0d8cc5e4] and Watchdog [watchdog-1] both essentially report that they cannot validate core technical controls (authz/session, upload safety, API hardening, dependency CVEs) or confirm any LLM integrations, so they cannot produce code-referenced findings. This convergence indicates that the current technical risk posture is largely unknown, not demonstrably safe.

The main conflict is interpretive: Guardian treats the system as high-impact and therefore non-deployable from a governance/compliance standpoint, issuing multiple critical/high findings (notably [guardian-1], [guardian-2], [guardian-3], [guardian-6], plus transparency/auditability gaps [guardian-7], [guardian-8]). In contrast, Sentinel and Watchdog assign low/info risk scores that reflect “unable to assess” rather than “meets requirements,” which can be read as a lower-risk posture despite the same missing-evidence premise. Overall, the synthesized posture is: technical security and AI-specific risks cannot be verified, while governance/compliance risks are already determinably critical based on the described use (automated moderation and authenticity judgments affecting public discourse).

Key gaps not fully covered include (1) explicit minimum-viable AWS/public API hardening controls and acceptance criteria (beyond requesting IaC), (2) adversarial ML robustness and poisoning/evasion threat modeling for ResNet/BERT pipelines independent of LLM usage, and (3) platform/ToS and legal-operational constraints around social-media monitoring and content handling. Addressing these gaps, along with providing the missing artifacts, is necessary to move from “unassessable/undocumented” to a defensible security and compliance posture.

top_findings: Insufficient source code provided for VeriMedia AI (Flask/Python application) security review
sentinel.summary: VeriMedia AI is described as a public-facing Flask/Python media verification platform integrating user uploads, REST APIs, social media integrations, and PostgreSQL on AWS, but no code or configuration artifacts were provided. Without the repository contents, security posture cannot be assessed against OWASP/WSTG/API Top 10 in a verifiable, file-and-line-referenced manner.
sentinel.recommendation: 1) Supply the Flask route handlers/auth/session code and upload pipeline code so authentication, authorization, CSRF/session cookie settings, and file upload constraints can be verified (Finding: Insufficient source code provided). 2) Provide dependency manifests (requirements.txt/lockfiles) to perform a CVE and insecure-version review (Finding: Insufficient source code provided). 3) Provide deployment/IaC and configuration/secret-management artifacts (Docker/K8s/Terraform/AWS configs) to assess

top_findings: No source code provided — cannot verify presence/absence of LLM integrations or assess OWASP LLM Top 10 risks
watchdog.summary: VeriMedia AI is described as a Flask/Python AI platform using non-LLM ML models (ResNet, BERT) for media verification. No source code was provided, so Watchdog cannot confirm any LLM usage (e.g., GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, LangChain/LlamaIndex) or produce evidence-based OWASP LLM Top 10 findings with file/line/snippet references.

However, the repository supplied contains no files at all, meaning there is no evidence of governance controls, privacy practices, model provenance, bias evaluation, transparency mechanisms, auditability, or regulatory alignment documentation.
